summary: Iter 69 Join Meeting 10-minute window — all 6 spec scenarios verified. Created pytest suite that signs up a fresh pro + student + non-participant student via /api/auth/signup + /api/auth/verify-otp (mock_otp), seeds 5 booked slots directly into db.interview_slots with varied start_at/end_at, then exercises GET /api/interviews/my-bookings and POST /api/interviews/{slot_id}/joined. 7/7 tests passed on the live preview backend.
test_cases_verified: TC1: 30-min-away slot → my-bookings returns join_enabled=false, meeting_url_hidden=true, meeting_url absent; POST /joined → 403 with '10 minutes before' message.
TC2: 5-min-away slot → join_enabled=true, meeting_url present, meeting_url_hidden absent; POST /joined → 200 and student id persisted in slot.joined_by.
TC3: Exactly 10-min-away slot → join_enabled=true (window opens at start-10min edge); POST /joined → 200.
TC4: In-progress slot (started 5m ago, ends in 20m) → join_enabled=true; pro POST /joined → 200.
TC5: Slot ended 5 min ago → join_enabled=false, meeting_url_hidden=true; POST /joined → 403 'This interview session has ended.'
TC6: Non-participant student POST /joined on someone else's slot → 403 'Not your session'.
critical_code_review_comments: interviews.py:396-407 join_enabled math is inclusive on both edges (window_start <= now <= window_end) — matches spec. Window_start = start_at - 10min, window_end = end_at.

mark_interview_joined enforces the same window (now < start-10m → 403 'Not Available'; now > end_at → 403 'session has ended') and checks participant membership before the time check — correct short-circuit ordering avoids leaking window info to non-participants.

meeting_url is popped only when join_enabled=false; meeting_url_hidden flag is set to True in that branch only. Response never sets meeting_url_hidden=false explicitly (spec allows 'absent or false'), which is fine.
my_bookings has a upcoming_only cutoff of now-2h (line 381) so the 'ended 5 min ago' slot is still surfaced — good.
Consider: /joined does NOT verify slot.status == 'booked' before adding to joined_by. Low risk since only participants can hit it, but a status guard would be defensive.
seed_data_creation: 5 interview_slots + 3 users (pro, student, other student) created and cleaned up in module teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iter69_join_window.py — signs up fresh disposable users each run (timestamp-suffixed emails) and cleans them up. Uses direct DB seed via motor for the interview_slots (bypasses booking's future-time guard).
